name: Robert Lignon McWhorter
major: Graduate School, Greek, Latin, German, A. B.

name: Ben Hand Askew
motto: "My heart hath melted to a lady's tears."
clubs: 1st Sergeant and Lieutenant Corps of Cadets

name: John Virgil Arrendale
motto: "A pearl may in a toad's head dwell."
enrolltime: Entered the Junior Class of the University of Georgia, Jan. 26, 1904

name: Eugene Marcus Baynes
motto: "As fresh as morning dew distilled on flowers."

name: Oliver Hazzard Bartow Bloodworth, Jr.
motto: "A form more fair, a face more sweet, Ne'er hath it been my lot to meet."
clubs: Vice-President and President Phi Kappa Society

name: Morris Henry Bernstein
motto: "With loads of learned lumber in his head."

name: Jos. P. Burke
motto: "To be great exceeds all power of face."

name: Edwin Williams Carson
motto: "There's no art to read the mind's construction in the face."

name: John Rutherford Fawcett
motto: "The empty vessel makes the greatest sound."
clubs: B. S. Representative U. of G. Commencement

name: John Alexander Copeland
motto: "An honest man—the noblest work of God."

name: George Lucas Clifton
clubs: Manager Senior Class Baseball, Football and Track Teams

name: Paul T. Harber
motto: "What may man within him hide, Tho' angel on the outward side."

name: Roderick Henry Hill
motto: "His virtues will plead like angels, trumpet-tongued."
clubs: Leader of Mandolin and Guitar Club, 1905

name: Preston W. Holtzendorf
motto: "One of the few who have a mission to cure incurable diseases."
clubs: Winner of William J. Bryan Prize, 1904

name: John R. Lee
motto: "My salad days, when I was green in judgment."

name: C. M. James
clubs: Senior Class Football and Baseball Teams

name: Joe Thorn Jackson
motto: "It warms me, it charms me, to mention but her name; It beats me, it beats me, and sets me all aflame."

name: Jacob Wailes Lewis
motto: "Much study is a weariness to the flesh."

name: Walter O. Marshburn
motto: "See what a grace is seated on this brow."

name: W. W. Patterson
motto: "Work is my recreation, the play of faculty."
major: A. B. Graduate of the University of Georgia, 1905
clubs: Winner of Prof. Barrow's Declamation Prize
Winner Sophomore Debaters' Medal Demosthenian Society

name: Robert Scott Newcomb
motto: "A February face so full of frost."

name: Louis Dugas McCleskey
motto: "I for one venerate a petticoat, A garment of mystical sublimity, No matter whether russet, silk or dimity."

name: Joel Branham Peniston
motto: "Impel the steps, increasing to pursue, Some fleeting good that mocks me with the view."

name: Chas. C. Pratt
motto: "He never needs the screen of lies, His inward bosom to disguise."

name: Dudley Walton Reynolds
motto: "Be great in act, as you have been in thought."

name: Charles Emory Smith
motto: "I am a part of all that I have met."

name: Claude Gordy Scarbrough
motto: "My only books were woman's looks, And follies all they taught me."

name: Horace B. Ritchie
motto: "My nature is that I incline to hope rather than fear."

name: Geo. M. Telford
motto: "God shall be my stay, my guide and lantern to my feet."

name: H. W. Telford
motto: "Oh, sane sincere; In action faithful and in honor clear."

name: Albert Edward Thornton, Jr.
motto: "On his brow shame is ashamed to sit."

name: Daniel Walter Yarbrough
motto: "Man wants but little here below, nor wants that little long."

name: S. Usher, U. P. L.
motto: "My words fly upward, my thoughts remain below."

name: Charlie Usher, U. P. L.
motto: "Labor with what zeal we will, something still remains undone."

name: Edwin N. Cobb
motto: "Like a man to double business bound, I stand in pause where I should first begin, and both neglect."

name: William Harold Lewis
motto: "And make us lose the good we oft' might win, by fearing to attempt."

name: Wesley Turnell Hanson
motto: "Shall never sag with doubt, nor shake with fear."

name: J. Claude Upshaw
motto: "I profess not talk, only this, That each man do his best."

name: William Anderson Daniel
motto: “By heaven, I do love! and it hath taught me to rhyme and be melancholy.”

name: Waverly Fairman
motto: “This man was never known to hurry.”

name: Rodney S. Cohen
motto: “A full blown Cupid, very much admired.”
clubs: Editor-in-Chief and Local Editor “Red and Black”
Associate Editor and Ass't Business Manager “The Georgian”
President and Vice-President University German Club

name: Francis L. Dancy
clubs: Charter Member Theta Lambda Phi (legal) Fraternity

name: George Washington Harben
clubs: Parliamentarian and Impromptu Debater Demosthenian Society

name: A. L. Hardy
motto: "I would my horse had the speed of thy tongue, and was so good a continuer."

name: James Virgil Pool
motto: "In time, the savage bull doth bear the yoke."

name: Cleveland Reuel Pierce
motto: "Wise from the top of his head up."

name: Virlyn Branham Moore
motto: "He will maintain his argument as well as any military man in the world."

name: G. A. Rubenstein
motto: "The glass of fashion and the mould of form."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Georgia Law Debating Society

name: John Eayres Davis Yonge
motto: "Have done, have done, here comes a gentleman."
major: M. S. Alabama Polytechnic Institute; B. L. University of Georgia, 1905
clubs: Exchange Editor and Editor-in-Chief "Red and Black"
Secretary and President Georgia Law Debating Society

name: Clarence Virdre Youmans
motto: "How much in love with himself; and that, without a rival."
enrolltime: Entered Law Class of '05 in September, 1904

name: Leon Abraham Hargreaves
motto: "In war was never lion caged more fierce."

name: Joseph Lumpkin Hull
motto: "God in mercy, so deal in my soul, As I in duty love my king and country."
